overview: Sultai Midrange aims to out-value opponents through resilient threats, efficient interaction, and late-game lock pieces, leveraging card advantage to outlast aggressive and combo decks while grinding down control strategies. It wins by controlling the early game, establishing a board presence with Abhorrent Oculus, and closing out games with Subtlety-protected threats or Sink into Stupor.
win_conditions: Sink into Stupor is the primary finisher, locking the opponent out of their deck or ending the game outright.
Subtlety-protected threats (like Abhorrent Oculus or other midrange creatures) can also close out games.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one 1-2 mana interaction (discard or spot removal), and a 2-3 drop threat or card advantage.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on 1-lands, 3+ lands with no early plays, or hands completely lacking interaction against aggro/combo.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a 1-mana interaction or pass.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast a 2-drop (removal, discard, or early threat).
Turn 3: Deploy Abhorrent Oculus if mana is open, or use interaction to protect it.
Prioritize untapped lands for flexibility and to enable Subtlety or Sink into Stupor later.
mid_game: Preserve mana for interaction until threats are committed.
Use Abhorrent Oculus to stabilize hand size and board.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without protection
Subtlety should be saved for key threats or to enable a surprise attack.
Manage graveyard by using Surgical Extraction or Rest in Peace when needed.
Commit threats when you have card advantage or removal ready.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or spot removal.
Use Subtlety on your most resilient threat or to protect a Sink into Stupor cast.
late_game: Sink into Stupor is the primary finisher, locking the opponent out of their deck or ending the game outright.
Subtlety-protected threats (like Abhorrent Oculus or other midrange creatures) can also close out games.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent has no sweepers, and you can protect your threats with Subtlety or removal.
Also commit when you can safely cast Sink into Stupor and the opponent lacks graveyard hate or disruption.

sequencing_tips: Abhorrent Oculus is fragile to sweepers but excels at generating card advantage and board presence.
Subtlety is a versatile tool—use it to protect threats, enable surprise attacks, or protect Sink into Stupor.
Sink into Stupor is a game-ending lock but vulnerable to graveyard hate
Avoid overcommitting to the board without protection, and always track opponent's mana and hand size to time your plays optimally.

matchups.advice: Use Subtlety to protect key spells, cast threats in response to counterspells, or use multiple threats to out-value counters. Sink into Stupor is often uncounterable or worth the risk due to its game-ending potential.
Apply early pressure with interaction, use Surgical Extraction to disrupt mana dorks, and stabilize with Abhorrent Oculus. Avoid trading inefficiently; out-value them with card advantage.
Prioritize early interaction and discard to slow the pace. Use Abhorrent Oculus to stabilize and draw into answers. Subtlety can protect a key threat to close out the game quickly.
Out-value them with card advantage (Oculus, discard). Use Subtlety to protect threats from spot removal. Sink into Stupor can lock them out of their own game plan.

Card advantage wins. Use Subtlety on your most resilient threat or to protect a Sink into Stupor. Prioritize graveyard hate and Spell Pierce to disrupt their resources. Win by out-drawing and out-resolving.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Boros Charm, Swords to Plowshares), spot removal, and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for spells vs. threats.
